title: That time my Discord server has been raided because of a dumb mistake.
author: By Pilot Alex
description: I used to have a Discord server a little while ago with my friends, it was a private server with only 4-5 people in it. But things started to get messy when I developed a Discord bot that leaked our server secret key.
content: [The begining] Everyone makes mistakes right? Well, I once made a big mistake, let me explain what happened to my Discord server in this post.
[The begining] This happened in 2020, I had a discord server that was used to talk with some of my friends. 5 people were in the server.

[The begining] And as a developer, I wanted to create a Discord server bot using JavaScript/NodeJs. So I started to look at some tutorials, create my Discord dev account, got my keys and started to code.
[The begining] The library I used was DiscordJS. It was great and easy.
[First error...] This is where I will make my first big mistake, coding the bot in an online public code editor. Something similar to Repl.it named 'Glitch': https://glitch.com/
[First error...] Don't ask me why I had this bad idea, I followed a tutorial on creating a Discord bot and the guy used this editor, so I mean...
[First error...] So what happened is that I hard coded the private bot key in the main file, yep, visible to anyone. It looked like something like this:
[First error...] Then I logged the bot using something like:
[First error...] Then, I hosted my blog on Heroku for free (wich isn't the case anymore 😒). And I let the public code on Glitch.
[The fail] Of course, a little while after my server got raided. Every channels where renamed, then deleted, the server icon picture was changed, messages were deleted, roles too.
Well then I quiclky realised this was because of me/my bot. I eventually decided to delete the server.
[What I could have done] - Don't use a public IDE for coding.
[What I could have done] - Use Environment Variables in NodeJs with process.env
[What I could have done] TOKEN = 'my-key-here'; // .env file
[What I could have done] client.login(process.env.TOKEN); // index.js
[What I could have done] The .env file should be added to the gitignore so it's not pushed to the GitHub repository.
[Wrapping up] Well this was a great learning experience of course, I lost my server but I learnt a lot, and that's the most important thing.
